title: TraceEvent
description:
Cross-SDK contract for one event in an agent, tool, or LLM execution trace. The Terrene Foundation's language-neutral source of truth for `kailash.diagnostics.TraceEvent` (Python) and kailash-rs `trace_event::TraceEvent` (Rust).

This schema is referenced verbatim by both SDKs. Changes to the contract require cross-SDK coordination before landing in either repo per `rules/eatp.md` § Cross-SDK Alignment and `rules/cross-sdk-inspection.md` MUST Rule 3.

canonicalization.timestamp_format: RFC 3339 with explicit +00:00 UTC offset; never Z
canonicalization.fingerprint_algorithm: SHA-256 of the UTF-8 bytes of the canonical JSON, hex-encoded lowercase
canonicalization.comparison: hmac.compare_digest (Python) / subtle::ConstantTimeEq (Rust). Never == on hex digests (timing side-channel).
canonicalization.cross_sdk_note: Python and Rust MUST produce byte-identical canonical JSON and byte-identical SHA-256 fingerprints for the same logical input. Paired with `schemas/audit-anchor.v1.json` (kailash-rs#449) — same canonicalization contract.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event (UUID or equivalent).

event_type.description: Enumerated event type. New values require cross-SDK coordination before landing in either SDK.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 timestamp with explicit +00:00 UTC offset. Z-form is BLOCKED to keep the byte-level form stable across SDKs.

run_id.description: Correlation identifier for the enclosing agent/tool run. Every event in one run shares the same run_id.

agent_id.description: Identifier of the agent that emitted the event. In PACT-native usage this is a D/T/R role address (e.g. 'D1-R1-D2-R2'); in kaizen-agents-native usage this is an agent UUID.

cost_microdollars.description: Cost of the operation in integer microdollars (1 USD = 1_000_000 microdollars). Integer-only to prevent float-accumulation drift across emitters. Cross-SDK aligned with `kaizen.cost.tracker` + kailash-rs#38.

parent_event_id.description: Optional parent event for nested spans. Used to reconstruct the event tree (e.g. llm.call.end under agent.step under agent.run.start).

trace_id.description: Optional OpenTelemetry trace identifier. Allows correlation with external OTel-aware observability backends.

span_id.description: Optional OpenTelemetry span identifier paired with trace_id.

tenant_id.description: Optional tenant identifier for multi-tenant isolation. When populated, downstream sinks SHOULD partition storage/metrics by tenant. See `rules/tenant-isolation.md`.

envelope_id.description: Optional PACT governance envelope identifier. Enables per-envelope consumption rollups in downstream diagnostics (pairs with `CostTracker.record(envelope_id=...)`).

tool_name.description: For tool.call.start / tool.call.end events: the invoked tool's identifier.

llm_model.description: For llm.call.start / llm.call.end events: the model identifier (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7'). Never hardcode — read from .env per `rules/env-models.md`.

prompt_tokens.description: Token count for the prompt side of an LLM call. Null for non-LLM events.

completion_tokens.description: Token count for the completion side of an LLM call. Null for non-LLM events.

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock duration of the operation in milliseconds. Present on *.end events; null on *.start events.

status.description: Terminal status of the operation. Present on *.end events; null on *.start events.

payload_hash.description: SHA-256 fingerprint of the raw payload when the payload contains classified values. Format: 'sha256:<8 hex chars>'. See `rules/event-payload-classification.md` §2 — the 8-hex-char length is the cross-SDK contract for forensic correlation without rainbow-table reversibility.

payload.description: Optional opaque payload. Emitters MUST apply `rules/event-payload-classification.md` rules before populating this field — classified string PKs hash to `payload_hash` (above) and are EXCLUDED from `payload`. Schema validation of payload contents is the adapter's responsibility, not the core protocol's.
